content:
- task finalized successfully: task file marked completed with passes true, Ralph task switch executed, commit created, docs post-commit publish succeeded, and git push completed
- final repo commit on master: 0ef8e74
- notable product fixes that closed the last regressions: generic switchover requests now resolve to a specific target immediately, storage-stalled primaries stay fenced after lease release, and operator-visible primary resolution only blocks insufficient sampling when corroboration is too weak

- Read previous Choose-Task progress log and loaded `.ralph/current_tasks.md` plus `.ralph/current_tasks_done.md`.

- Now inspecting each active task file for bug markers and blockers before selecting the next task path for `.ralph/current_task.txt`.

- Checked all active candidates from `.ralph/current_tasks.md` and verified none of them had `<blocked_by>` tags, so none are blocked by the done-task registry.
- There are no active bug tasks in the current backlog. Among unblocked failing non-bug tasks, the highest priority candidate is `.ralph/tasks/story-greenfield-cucumber-ha-harness/05-task-produce-ha-refactor-option-artifacts-email-review-and-stop-ralph.md` with `<priority>high</priority>`; the operator-ergonomics tasks are all `<priority>low</priority>`.
- Wrote that task path into `.ralph/current_task.txt` and am stopping immediately per the task-selection instructions.

- narrowed worker.rs to the exact member publication and trust flow: stale local member deletion, self member slot write, watch/snapshot refresh, stale leader cleanup, trust evaluation, and DcsState publication
- next step is to return a concise but precise summary with file references for member publication, quorum heuristics, partial-truth publishing, and the HA scenarios implied by tests/ha.rs

- read prior progress log and inspected runtime/node.rs, ha/worker.rs, ha/decide.rs, ha/decision.rs, ha/lower.rs, and ha/process_dispatch.rs
- established current live HA loop as observe -> decide -> reconcile -> publish ha state -> execute actions, and identified that sender-side dedup currently hinges on deterministic process_job_id(scope,self,tick,index,action)
- checking whether startup split/bridging helpers still exist in live code or only in disabled test references before replying with precise file anchors

- confirmed the repo currently has a split HA model: live worker path uses decide.rs + reconcile.rs + process_dispatch.rs, while decision.rs + lower.rs define a typed decision/effect pipeline referenced elsewhere but not by src/ha/worker.rs
- current startup live path is run_node_from_config -> process_defaults_from_config -> ensure_start_paths -> run_workers; startup-planning helpers appear only as references inside a disabled #[cfg(all(test, any()))] block in src/runtime/node.rs
- final response will explicitly separate live control flow from dead/test-gated startup-planning artifacts so the redesign doc cites the right anchors

- created first real redesign artifact: artifacts/01-regime-first-ha-reconciliation.md
- corrected the design text after code exploration showed the live startup path is run_node_from_config -> run_workers, while the old explicit startup planner only survives in stale disabled references
- make test completed green with 309 passed and 3 leaky; waiting on make test-long before finalizing the diagnostic section and emailing this one artifact

- make test-long finished with 25 passed, 1 failed, 4 skipped; the failure was ha_all_nodes_stopped_then_two_nodes_restarted_then_final_node_rejoins timing out waiting for one primary across the two restarted fixed nodes
- this failure is directly relevant to the regime-first design because it highlights ambiguous restart authority after full cluster outage
- next steps are to update the artifact diagnostics, fix the task file's inaccurate completed/final-phase claims, sanity-check reply.sh, email the artifact in full, then stop immediately
